transcript:
1
00:00:10,020 --> 00:01:00,600
[SPEAKER_00] So we will now officially start book number two. So normally whenever we would start any book in the aalim course and our teachers, we would make a dua that Allah Ta'ala allows us to finish the book with khair and aafiya and allow us to benefit from the book. So we can make dua inshaAllah. Arabic Okay, so now let us start inshaAllah the first lesson. Al-Darsul Likah. So before

2
00:01:00,600 --> 00:01:56,379
[SPEAKER_00] we start the lesson, a brief overview of some of the important concepts that we will be discussing in this. So in this first lesson, one important thing that will be introduced is inna. So one important thing that will be introduced here is inna. So this is a new family that we are now being introduced to. Right from the get go, right from the beginning, we are starting a new family. So. Okay. Aaliyah.

3
00:02:00,959 --> 00:02:50,879
[SPEAKER_00] إِنَّ Has anyone ever seen this before in the Qur'an? Everybody, right? Because it comes every page in the Qur'an. You'll probably find at least one of these, right? So إِنَّ is a very, very common word in the Qur'an. So it's part of a family that is called... Again, with the term, it's not extremely important. If you can't remember the term, not a big deal, right? But know what it does. Know what the purpose of it is. Like a tsunami.

4
00:03:05,980 --> 00:04:00,319
[SPEAKER_00] أَلْأَحْرُفُ الْمُشَبَّهَةُ بِالْفِعْلِ That's the name of the family. We've started a new family already from the second book. So it's إِنَّ, right? So what does it mean? So this is a حرف. إِنَّ is a حرف, right? So أَحْرُفُ is the plural of حرف. أَحْرُفُ الْمُشَبَّهَةُ بِالْفِعْلِ Those أحرف, those حرفs, حرف that are similar to a فعل. Now what that similarity is, again, that's a longer topic and something I don't want you to get confused on, but this is just the name of this family. Now if it is too confusing and too long for you, then an easier name for this family is... أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّ أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّ So إِنَّ is... You can say the head of the family. And

5
00:04:00,319 --> 00:05:00,100
[SPEAKER_00] then the rest, all the other of its family members are called أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّ The sisters of إِنَّ That's literally what it says in some books, including this book. So if you look at page, for example, وَلَيْكُمْ سَلَامٌ If you look at page... Where'd it go? Yeah. Look at page 10. On the top it says, أَدْخِلْ لَعَلَّ عَلَى الْجُمِلِ أَذِيَةً لَعَلْمًا بِأَنَّ لَعَلَّ مِنْ أَخَوَاتِ إِنَّ لَعَلَّ is part of the family of أَخَوَاتِ إِنَّ So... Normally at this point, our teachers would right away tell us all the أَخَوَاتِ إِنَّ, the sisters of إِنَّ. But I think, I don't want to confuse you guys. So we'll just be introduced to them gradually, the sisters of إِنَّ. For now, let's just learn إِنَّ itself. Okay? So إِنَّ, first of all, what does it mean? It doesn't have

6
00:05:00,100 --> 00:06:00,300
[SPEAKER_00] a particular meaning itself, it's just for more emphasis, when you want to emphasize something more. So that's why normally in translations, you usually see إِنْدِيْد or وَرَلِي Right? So, we don't normally use these words anyways in speech, right? Have you ever heard somebody saying وَرَلِي, I'm going to the park or something? No, right? So it's not normally used in speech. The purpose is just to emphasize something. So, that's the main purpose for تَأْكِيد. That's the word in Arabic. تَأْكِيد I think this is similar in Urdu, right? تَأْكِيد There's that word in Urdu too, تَأْكِيد تَأْكِيد To emphasize something. Right? So تَأْكِيد That's the purpose of إِنَّ And that's in terms of the meaning. Now what about in terms of the إِعْرَاب What difference does it make in terms of فَتْحَكَسْرَضَمَّةَ So first of all, one thing to know is إِنَّ

7
00:06:00,300 --> 00:07:00,100
[SPEAKER_00] It only comes with one type of sentence. So I don't know if you remember, but once upon a time we learned that there are two types of sentences. There's جُمْلَ إِسْمِيَة and جُمْلَ فَعْلِيَة Do you guys remember that? It's simple. There are sentences which start with an إِسْم and sentences which start with a فَعْل فَعْل is what? A verb. So some sentences start with an إِسْم Some sentences start with a verb. So this only enters upon the first category which is الجُمْلَ الإِسْمِيَة So now that means that, for example الله غفور غفور وحيم Allah is merciful as Allah is forgiving and merciful When you

8
00:07:00,100 --> 00:07:58,279
[SPEAKER_00] add إِنَّ to the mix So over here is ضَمَّ This is the مُبْتَرَ This is the subject This is the predicate Allah is forgiving and merciful Now once you add إِنَّ to the mix then what happens? إِنَّ makes the word the مُبْتَرَ the subject, the first word فَتْحَ So you say إِنَّ اللهَ غفور رحيم غفور رحيم غفور رحيم So that's what إِنَّ does It makes that first word that first the مُبْتَرَ into a فَتْحَ مُبْتَرَ is the issue? Yeah, that's what it's called I'm not going to get into the technicality yet if I really understand the concept Everybody got it? So, Hufaald Can you give me some examples of إِنَّ that comes like this where it makes the word فَتْحَ

9
00:08:00,360 --> 00:09:00,019
[SPEAKER_00] That's different because الَّذِينَ is one of those words الَّذِينَ is one of those words that don't change It's مَبْنِي so it doesn't change Good إِنَّ الدِّينَ If إِنَّ did not exist over there it would be الدِّينُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الْإِسْلَامُ That would be the sentence But because إِنَّ came in for extra emphasis then it became إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الْإِسْلَامُ Okay Another example of Hufaald إِنَّ المُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْمَاتِ Good إِنَّ المُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْمَاتِ But that I don't want to confuse anybody because that's a different type of word that when it becomes فَتْحَ it just becomes يَا إِنَّ المُسْلِمُونَ Because normally it's مُسْلِمُونَ But when it becomes فَتْحَ it just becomes مُسْلِمِينَ But that's a different I don't want to confuse anybody We're just talking about the normal letter for now إِنَّ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ Good إِنَّ أَصْحَابَ الْجَنَّةِ الْيَوْمَ فِي شُغْلٍ فَكْيُونَ So if إِنَّ was not

10
00:09:00,019 --> 00:10:00,080
[SPEAKER_00] there it would have been أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ Like in the other ayahs of the Qur'an Allah says أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌ مُسْتَقَرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلًا So because there's no إِنَّ it's stated as a ضَمَّ Any other examples, Muhafaz? إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِينَ Very good, mashaAllah إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِينَ Without إِنَّ it would have just been اللَّهُ مَعَ الصَّابِينَ But because إِنَّ came in then it became إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِينَ فَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ فَأَخُوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ Good إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ If إِنَّ was not there أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ Good Two more examples and we'll continue إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ Good, very good, mashaAllah Otherwise it would have been اللَّهُ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ إِنَّ اللَّهَ

11
00:10:00,080 --> 00:11:00,159
[SPEAKER_00] وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ Good, yeah إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٍ If إِنَّ was not there it would have just been اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٍ And many places it is like that وَاللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٍ Very good So we get the idea, right إِنَّ makes the word after it It doesn't always have to be the word after it That's the key thing though It makes the مُبْتَدَ The thing that was originally the مُبْتَدَ It makes it فَتْحَ now The thing that was the subject It makes it فَتْحَ Okay So that's the general rule for إِنَّ And it gives emphasis, right Yeah And when you go further into the Arabic language The ulema explain that there are certain scenarios In which you use إِنَّ Especially for example when the person you are speaking to Is in doubt about what you're saying Or if they reject what you're saying They don't believe you So you emphasize,

12
00:11:00,259 --> 00:12:00,360
[SPEAKER_00] you say إِنَّ To make, basically to Emphasize your point and say You know, you should believe me And then you're emphasizing it more Right So many times it's used in that type of context Okay, so that's one of the most important things That we learn in this lesson Is about إِنَّ It's a حرف مشبه بالفعل Then Another thing we learn is that This is if إِنَّ comes in a regular sentence Where there's no ضمير Where there's no pronoun Let's say it comes in a sentence in which there's a pronoun Then what happens? Then if you turn to page seven Look at page seven Tamreen number three Are you guys on there? So look Whenever you have to say إِنَّ and then هو You don't say إِنَّ هو No, you just connect it together It becomes إِنَّ هو Right So for example Where does إِنَّ هو come in the Quran? Many places,

13
00:12:00,379 --> 00:13:00,179
[SPEAKER_00] so many places, right? إِنَّ هو هو الغفور الرحيم Right إِنَّ هو مع المغادرة الصالحة Good, yeah إِنَّ هو مع المغادرة الصالحة Countless places it comes إِنَّ هو Alright So إِنَّها Same thing If you have هي إِنَّ plus هي equals إِنَّها Okay It's simple because we already learned these attached pronouns All you have to do is attach the pronouns to إِنَّ itself Understood? So إِنَّ plus أنت equals إِنَّك إِنَّ plus أنت equals إِنَّك Right إِنَّهُم Again, all these, pretty much all these Come in the Quran إِنَّها Where does إِنَّها come? وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى الْخَاشِعِينَ إِنَّهَا بَقْرَةٌ Yeah, good, the story of the cow إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَا فَارِضٌ وَلَا بَكْرٌ Good Then what about إِنَّكَ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَلِيمُ

14
00:13:00,179 --> 00:13:59,980
[SPEAKER_00] الْحَجِيمُ Good سُبْحَانَكَ لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا إِلَّا مَا عَلَّمْتَنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَجِيمُ Indeed, you are the most knowledgeable and the most wise Good What about إِنَّكِ I'll give you a clue, it's in the story of Yusuf إِنَّكِ كُنْتِ مِنَ الْخَاطِئِينَ Good, mashallah إِنَّكِ كُنْتِ مِنَ الْخَاطِئِينَ Right, when The wife of the woman who was trying to seduce Yusuf Sorry, the husband of the woman who was trying to seduce Yusuf Found out that she was the one at fault Then he said إِنَّكِ كُنْتِ مِنَ الْخَاطِئِينَ Surely you were the one who was mistaken, basically خَاطِئِينَ Then أنا If you add أنا to it, it becomes إِنِّي إِنِّي or إِنَّنِي It's all the same thing Don't get confused, right? Some place Allah says إِنَّنِي أَنَ اللَّهُ

15
00:14:00,480 --> 00:15:00,620
[SPEAKER_00] And other places it says إِنِّي أَنَ اللَّهُ So إِنَّنِي and إِنِّي are the exact same thing That extra noon is just there for pronunciation purposes But there's no bearing on the meaning So إِنِّي and إِنَّنِي They both come in the Qur'an and they both mean the same thing In these cases The فَتْحَ is taken away by the دَمَاين Yeah, exactly, good point So that rule we just learnt That the word after إِنَّ becomes فَتْحَ That doesn't apply here with the ضَمِير Because these pronouns They are مَبْنِمِين They don't change based on whatever comes before it They only change based on who is being referred to So therefore They don't become فَتْحَ just because they're after إِنَّ In that case إِنَّ It doesn't make anything فَتْحَ at that point When it has a ضَمِير, a pronoun attached to it Everybody understand? Yes? No? So

16
00:15:00,620 --> 00:16:00,200
[SPEAKER_00] You see إِنَّ here We already learnt the rule that إِنَّ makes فَتْحَ But then if you have إِنَّهُ Then This takes up إِنَّ So then now it's not going to make anything فَتْحَ You get it? So for example إِنَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ So now This is already taking up the إِنَّ's effect So now إِنَّ won't make anything فَتْحَ This will just be غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ Yeah? Yeah Yeah Is there a case where the words that come after it غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ Do they change as well to like part of your statement? Not with إِنَّ There's another word called كَانَ So كَانَ does the opposite of إِنَّ إِنَّ makes the first the مُبْتَدَ فَتْحَ But كَانَ makes the خَانَ فَتْحَ The end part فَتْحَ For example وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورً رَحِيمً وَكَانَ

17
00:16:00,200 --> 00:16:59,919
[SPEAKER_00] اللَّهُ غَفُورً رَحِيمً Got it? So كَانَ is the opposite We're going to get there I think the next lesson actually is كَانَ So كَانَ is a word that does that Right? But not إِنَّ إِنَّ إِنَّ only changes the مُبْتَدَ The first part of the sentence The subject And the predicate stays as دَمَّ As بِشْ Okay? Okay, so that's another thing, إِنَّ, then if you add a pronoun to it, it becomes إِنَّهُ, إِنَّهَا, إِنَّكَ, إِنَّكِ, إِنِّي, إِنَّنِي, إِنَّهُمْ, إِنَّهُنَّ, إِنَّكُمْ, إِنَّكُنَّ, إِنَّنَا, إِنَّا. Right, so I think, I can probably think of every single one of these come up in the Qur'an, right? إِنَّنِي and الله, right? And إِنَّهُمْ, إِنَّهُمْ, where is إِنَّهُمْ in the Qur'an? Very good, mashallah. أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَكِلَّا يَشْعُرُونَ Indeed, they are the مُفْسِدُونَ, the corruptors.

18
00:17:00,519 --> 00:18:00,259
[SPEAKER_00] إِنَّهُنَّ رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ Yes, very good. رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضْلَلْنَا كَثِيرًا مِنَ النَّاسِ Very good. ثَنْ إِنَّكُمْ إِنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ قَوْمًا فَاسِقِينَ Right? Then, أَنْ إِنَّكُنَّ This might not be there. What do you think? Yeah, I don't think this is in the Qur'an. But other than that, all these are in the Qur'an. إِنَّا إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَلْنَا الذِّكْرَةُ Right? إِنَّنَا وَإِنَّنَا لَفِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا تَدْعُونَا إِلَيْهِمُ رِيبٌ So all of these come in the Qur'an, so that's why again, we should know this very well. إِنَّا is very, very common in the Qur'an. So it's part of this family called الْأَحْرَفُ الْمُشَبَّةُ بِالْفَرْضِ or أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّا And the rule is that it makes the مُبْتَرَة, the first part of the sentence, فَتْحَة Understood? And what does it mean? It gives emphasis. Indeed, surely, or verily, you can translate it any of these ways. Surely is probably more,

19
00:18:00,380 --> 00:19:00,119
[SPEAKER_00] the most commonly, like, easily understandable for somebody. So that's a good word we can use. Now, any other specific, let's see. Yeah. The other ones, this is the main concept, the other ones, we'll just explain it as it comes up, inshallah. Alright. Who wants to read? Okay. I'm about to tell you, may I have one more? Anybody else? Alright. Go ahead. As-salamu alaykum. As-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuhu. As-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuhu. How are you, Ustaz? Maybe you're doing well? Okay. Sorry, I forgot to add this. لَعَلَّ So لَعَلَّ is, so we were

20
00:19:00,119 --> 00:20:00,000
[SPEAKER_00] officially learned one member of the family of أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّا وَأَحْفُرُ الْأُشَبَى بِالفَعَلِ which is إِنَّ That's one. But in this very lesson is number two as well, which is, لَعَلَّ لَعَلَّ is also part of the same exact family. So the rule is exactly the same. The only thing that changes is the meaning. Okay. لَعَلَّ means perhaps. Okay. Perhaps. Or it seems like, something along those lines. إِنَّ and لَعَلَّ are part of which family? This family. There's two possible names you can give it. أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّا وَأَحْفُرُ الْأُشَبَى بِالفَعَلِ Or أَخَوَاتُ إِنَّا The sisters of إِنَّا. That's the easier way to understand it. So إِنَّ and لَعَلَّ. So now, لَعَلَّ means perhaps or yeah. And the same rule apply with the pronouns. So over

21
00:20:00,000 --> 00:21:00,220
[SPEAKER_00] here he says, لَعَلَّكَ بِخَيْرٍ Perhaps you are okay. However, this gives more the meaning of, hopefully you are okay, basically. It's something which is not certain. لَعَلَّ means, it's something which is not certain, but it's something that you hope, you hope that it's going to happen, basically. Where's the فَتَّة come from? So because there's a pronoun, لَعَلَّكَ So it doesn't cause anything to become فَتْحَ Whenever there's a pronoun attached to it, then the فَتْحَ rule doesn't apply. Understood? Hopefully you're okay. Yeah. Hopefully you are okay. Yeah, exactly. Like how we say, I hope you are doing well. So it's like something like that. Okay. Continue. It's your turn, right? I think it's me. Yeah. For Hashim? No, but that is. Oh, no, that's it. الحمد لله وكيف حالك أنت يا حاشم أنا محبوك

22
00:21:00,220 --> 00:21:58,180
[SPEAKER_00] كتير يا حاشم إنك طولباً زكيباً ومجتهباً وضوء قلوباً أمين باكستان أنت أمين مني يا حاشم أمين باكستان أنت أمين مني يا حاشم Okay. Do you want to translate? Yeah, sure. الحمد لله and how are you, O Hashim? How are you, Hashim? I love you a lot. O Hashim, you are a smart student and hardworking. Okay. This is a new thing we're learning as well. Zou. So, what is Zou? Zou just means the possessor of or the owner of something. Okay. So, over here it says Zou and it's going to be مضاف to the word after it. So, the word after here is خلق.

23
00:22:01,759 --> 00:22:58,880
[SPEAKER_00] خلق is the singular version of أخلاق. Right. أخلاق we all know is? Manners. Manners, character. So, خلق is just the singular version of that. So, you are ذو خلق means that you have, you are a possessor of good character basically. Right. So, again, you don't have to translate it as you are a possessor of good character. You can just say you have good character like that. Right. So, where does ذو come in the Qur'an? Somebody give an example. Good. Good. والله ذو الفضل العظيم والله ذو الفضل العظيم والله ذو الفضل العظيم والله is the possessor of great bounties. Right. So, yeah. والله عزيز ذو انتقام ذو انتقام It's actually ذو انتقام but because نحمد الوصل we connected. So, ذو انتقام What does انتقام mean?

24
00:23:01,319 --> 00:23:59,880

title: Understanding the Harfu'l-Mashbihah: Inna and La'alla in Sentence Structure
summary: This session introduces fundamental concepts of Arabic grammar with a focus on the particles إِنَّ (inna) and لَعَلَّ (la'lla), their roles as Harfu'l-Mashbihah, and their impact on sentence structure. Students learn how these particles emphasize statements and alter the case endings of nouns, particularly in ismialiyeh sentences.
key_points: إِنَّ emphasizes the subject (مُبْتَدَأ) by giving it a fathah (فتح) instead of its default damma (ضمة).
Examples like إِنَّ اللهَ غفور رحيم demonstrate how إِنَّ changes the subject's case.
La'lla serves as an uncertainty indicator (e.g., 'perhaps'), similar to inna but with distinct contextual usage.
Attached pronouns with inna form variants like إِنَّك, إِنَّهِم, which don't alter subsequent word cases.
The lesson emphasizes practical Qur'anic examples to illustrate the particles' functions.
questions: [00:06:38] Q: What happens when inna is added to a sentence? — A: It makes the subject (مُبْتَدَأ) have a fathah instead of damma.
[00:21:07] Q: How does la'lla differ from inna? — A: La'lla indicates uncertainty, similar to 'perhaps'.
[00:34:36] Q: Why is the subject first when using 'am?' — A: The question's focus comes before the subject for grammatical structure.
